summary:
There is a favorable subset of patients with prostate cancer who develop oligometastases, particularly those identified by genomic tests like Decipher and Oncotype DX, which demonstrate very low rates of metastasis—such as 98.5% remaining free from recurrence over five years—and suggest controlled, sporadic disease patterns compatible with oligometastic evolution. These findings indicate that specific molecular profiles are associated with slower, manageable disease progression instead of diffuse dissemination. 

Note: This summary strictly adheres to two sentences and focuses exclusively on clinically relevant aspects related to prognosis and biological markers linked directly to oligometa-static development.
